## [09:07 - 12:13]

So when I think about my audiences, I'm thinking cold, warm and hot. Cold is people that haven't clicked, haven't engaged in any way, haven't bought. So as you can see here, relationship is I'm here for you and I'm actually starting that communication. Reporters, I understand you. I hear you and I'm here for you and relevance. Repor is then working that through and seeing if I can actually generate what you know, whatever my funnel is, start to build those goals there. And relevance is delivering those ads at the right time. So the right out of the right time. For example, if I've got a video ad funnel, I might deliver one video ad and then retarget that with another video ad. And then retarget people that view that with static ad or a carousel. evoked, people are more likely to take an interest in what you're selling. Most of the time when people say it's expensive, it means you haven't explained the value proposition well enough. So for example, some people don't even know they have a problem. So one of the great examples I like to use is Henry Ford. At no point did anyone say to him, you know what, we need to upgrade these horses and replace them with cars. So that's the kind of the that as the story goes, he didn't ask people what they wanted. He he made something that he believed they needed. Similar thing could be argued of Apple. No one at the time was even imagining they needed an iPod. Apple produced it and launched it. Now everyone needed an iPod. And Apple kind of led the way for many years with the iPad and things like that. They they created a solution for a problem people didn't really know they had. ## [18:18 - 21:19]

Facebook loves great data going into your website coming out of your account. So if I'm getting a so this is this is an example, if I'm getting a low CTR, let's say 0.8% or 1%, but the people that click are having a great experience on my site, my bounce rates are low, my conversion rates are high, that counts for way more than having like a 4% conversion click through rate on your ads, but really low site performance. So that's something to really, really think about when you're creating your ads. So for example, I've heard people talk about running traffic campaigns. They just want a pile of traffic from their audiences into the site. And then what they do is retarget them. The problem with that is you're sending a lot of bad signals to Facebook, especially if it's poor quality traffic. You know, the app, you can honestly, it's easy to create ads at great high click through rate. It really is. But it's really hard to find ads that convince people that your solution is right for them. And I think that's what I would really encourage you to think about here as well. Then going on to formats, this is just an indicative view of how I manage the different formats. This is guidance. This is not exactly what you should do. So for example, here you might talk a bit more with product videos with Wow and problem influencers potentially. So by associating yourself with an influencer, you can bring that problem to the fore and automatically the awareness shoots up as well. And also the trust. It could be testimonials or reviews. Generally, I do run them in my kind of mid funnel, but they can work top of funnel, especially when you've got really good visual review or testimonial and they're really breaking out the products. And it's genuine. You know, people can tell if it's a fake review. So make it genuine as well. And then also kind of info videos. What I talk about in your videos is, let's say you have a 60 to 90 or maybe 120 second video, which talks about a problem in a solution. So for example, when I'm doing lead generation, when I'm selling services, courses, coaching, things like that, I will often drop really short videos in and say, yes, you know, scaling Facebook ads is a common problem. These are the things that you need to look out right now. Now, if someone opts to view that video, I know that scaling is their problem. And if they watch, let's say 75% or more, they're really interesting finding out how to solve their scaling problem. So then I can build a funnel off the back of it. So that's just an idea there. And then as you go through, there's different things that you can look at for your content. FOMO, fear of missing out is obviously a big one to get people, but it often works far better when their intent is higher. And their awareness is better as well. Because if you start running FOMO here, they don't know who you are.
